--- title: "Unit 2: Indices" number-sections: true number-depth: 2 execute: echo: false --- ## 🎯 Learning Targets :::::::::::::::::: lt-card ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 1 ::: ::: lt-content **Apply Multiplication Rules** – Simplify expressions by adding powers when multiplying terms with the same base. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 2 ::: ::: lt-content **Apply Division Rules** – Simplify expressions by subtracting powers when dividing terms with the same base. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 3 ::: ::: lt-content **Master Power of a Power** – Use the rule to simplify expressions where a power is raised to another power. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 4 ::: ::: lt-content **Manage Coefficients** – Accurately multiply or divide numerical coefficients separately from the algebraic index laws. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 5 ::: ::: lt-content **Handle Negative Indices** – Correctly perform addition and subtraction of exponents when calculations involve negative numbers. ::: ::::: ::::: lt-item ::: lt-number 6 ::: ::: lt-content **Simplify Complex Fractions** – Combine multiple index laws to simplify multi-step algebraic fractions involving multiplication and division. ::: ::::: :::::::::::::::::: ## 🗂️ Formula Card :::::::::::: formula-card ### Algebraic Indices & Expansion Rules {.unnumbered} #### Laws of Indices (The Core Rules) {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-yellow **💡 Pro Tip** These rules ONLY apply when the **base** (the big letter) is the same. ::: ::: rule-group **Multiplication & Division** 1. **Multiplication**: Add the powers $\rightarrow$ $$x^a \times x^b = x^{a+b}$$ 2. **Division**: Subtract the powers $\rightarrow$ $$\frac{x^a}{x^b} = x^{a-b}$$ **Examples**: $$y^5 \times y^3 = y^{5+3} = y^8$$ $$10w^7 \div 2w^3 = (10 \div 2)w^{7-3} = 5w^4$$ ::: #### Power of a Power & Zero {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-blue **🎯 Memory Trick** Brackets mean multiply!\ Anything to the power of $0$ is a hero (it becomes $1$). ::: ::: rule-group **The Bracket Rule**: $$(x^a)^b = x^{a \times b} = x^{ab}$$ **Examples**: $$(k^3)^4 = k^{3 \times 4} = k^{12}$$ $$(5x^2)^3 = 5^3 \times x^{2 \times 3} = 125x^6$$ **The Zero Rule**: $$x^0 = 1$$ ::: #### Negative & Fractional Indices {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-pink **⚠️ Common Mistake** A negative power does NOT make the number negative; it makes it a reciprocal (fraction)! ::: ::: rule-group **Negative Powers**: $$x^{-a} = \frac{1}{x^a}$$\ **Fractional Powers**: $$x^{\frac{1}{n}} = \sqrt[n]{x}$$ **Examples**: $$4^{-2} = \frac{1}{4^2} = \frac{1}{16}$$ $$9^{\frac{1}{2}} = \sqrt{9} = 3$$ ::: #### Expanding Brackets with Indices {.unnumbered} ::: sticky-note-orange **📝 Quick Method** Multiply the "Big" numbers (coefficients), add the "Small" numbers (indices). ::: ::: rule-group **Single Brackets**: $$a(b + c) = ab + ac$$ **Examples**: $$3x^2(2x^3 + 4) = (3 \times 2)x^{2+3} + (3 \times 4)x^2 = 6x^5 + 12x^2$$ $$x^5(x^2 - x^{-3}) = x^{5+2} - x^{5-3} = x^7 - x^2$$ ::: #### Algebraic Fractions with Indices {.unnumbered} ::: rule-group **Simplifying Fractions**: 1. Simplify the numerator (top) first using multiplication laws. 2. Simplify the denominator (bottom). 3. Use the division law (subtract) to find the final result. **Example**: $$\frac{a^5 \times a^4}{a^2} = \frac{a^{5+4}}{a^2} = \frac{a^9}{a^2} = a^{9-2} = a^7$$ **Example with Coefficients**: $$\frac{(2x^3)^2}{4x^4} = \frac{4x^6}{4x^4} = 1x^{6-4} = x^2$$ ::: :::::::::::: ## 📝 Practice Problems :::::::::: panel-tabset ### 🟢 Easy ::::: {#exr-u2-1} ### Multiplication Rule Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $w^{2} \times w^{2}$ b) $n^{3} \times n^{3}$ c) $c^{7} \times c^{2}$ d) $w^{7} \times w^{6}$ e) $b^{6} \times b^{5}$ f) $a^{2} \times a^{2}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $w^{4}$ b) $n^{6}$ c) $c^{9}$ d) $w^{13}$ e) $b^{11}$ f) $a^{4}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Index notation Same base identification Addition of integers
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-2} ### Multiplication Rule — Three Terms Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $m^{3} \times m^{6} \times m^{6}$ b) $a^{6} \times a^{3} \times a^{6}$ c) $y^{3} \times y^{5} \times y^{6}$ d) $n^{2} \times n^{3} \times n^{5}$ e) $x^{4} \times x^{3} \times x^{3}$ f) $x^{2} \times x^{2} \times x^{5}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $m^{15}$ b) $a^{15}$ c) $y^{14}$ d) $n^{10}$ e) $x^{10}$ f) $x^{9}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Index notation Same base identification Addition of integers Multi step simplification
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-7} ### Division Rule Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $w^{8} \div w^{2}$ b) $p^{10} \div p^{4}$ c) $x^{5} \div x^{2}$ d) $y^{7} \div y^{3}$ e) $a^{9} \div a^{4}$ f) $c^{8} \div c^{2}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $w^{6}$ b) $p^{6}$ c) $x^{3}$ d) $y^{4}$ e) $a^{5}$ f) $c^{6}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Index notation Same base identification Subtraction of integers
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-10} ### Power of a Power Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $(k^{2})^{2}$ b) $(a^{5})^{4}$ c) $(b^{3})^{3}$ d) $(m^{6})^{5}$ e) $(c^{5})^{3}$ f) $(n^{5})^{3}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $k^{4}$ b) $a^{20}$ c) $b^{9}$ d) $m^{30}$ e) $c^{15}$ f) $n^{15}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Index notation Multiplication of integers Power of power law
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-21} ### Zero Index Evaluate. a) $1$ b) $1$ c) $1$ d) $1$ e) $1$ f) $1$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $1$ b) $1$ c) $1$ d) $1$ e) $1$ f) $1$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Zero exponent rule Index notation
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🟡 Medium ::::: {#exr-u2-3} ### Multiplication with Coefficients Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $4b^{6} \times 4b^{4}$ b) $5a^{2} \times 6a^{5}$ c) $6b^{7} \times 4b^{6}$ d) $6x^{7} \times 3x^{2}$ e) $3a^{2} \times 4a^{3}$ f) $5b^{5} \times 4b^{7}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $16b^{10}$ b) $30a^{7}$ c) $24b^{13}$ d) $18x^{9}$ e) $12a^{5}$ f) $20b^{12}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Index notation Coefficient multiplication Addition of integers Separating coefficients from indices
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-5} ### Combining Same Powers — (ab)ⁿ = aⁿ×bⁿ Write as a single power by expressing each number as a prime power. a) $4 \times 49$ b) $32 \times 243$ c) $32 \times 243$ d) $8 \times 27$ e) $9 \times 25$ f) $32 \times 243$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $14^{2}$ b) $6^{5}$ c) $6^{5}$ d) $6^{3}$ e) $15^{2}$ f) $6^{5}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Prime factorisation Perfect square recognition Perfect cube recognition Power distribution law
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-6} ### Multiplication — Multi-variable Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $3c^{5}t^{3} \times 3c^{5}t^{2}$ b) $2w^{2}x^{4} \times 3w^{4}x^{3}$ c) $4b^{5}n^{4} \times 3b^{5}n^{2}$ d) $3n^{5}c^{3} \times 4n^{5}c^{2}$ e) $5c^{2}t^{2} \times 2c^{2}t^{2}$ f) $2w^{5}y^{3} \times 5w^{5}y$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $9c^{10}t^{5}$ b) $6w^{6}x^{7}$ c) $12b^{10}n^{6}$ d) $12n^{10}c^{5}$ e) $10c^{4}t^{4}$ f) $10w^{10}y^{4}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Multi variable expressions Coefficient multiplication Addition of integers Grouping like terms
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-8} ### Division with Coefficients Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $12n^{5} \div 4n^{3}$ b) $20p^{7} \div 4p^{2}$ c) $12a^{5} \div 4a^{2}$ d) $4a^{8} \div 2a^{3}$ e) $9k^{7} \div 3k^{2}$ f) $12w^{10} \div 3w^{5}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $3n^{2}$ b) $5p^{5}$ c) $3a^{3}$ d) $2a^{5}$ e) $3k^{5}$ f) $4w^{5}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Coefficient division Subtraction of integers Separating coefficients from indices
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-9} ### Division — Multi-variable Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $\dfrac{12p^{6}w^{3}}{3p^{4}w}$ b) $\dfrac{4w^{8}x^{3}}{2w^{4}x^{3}}$ c) $\dfrac{4b^{3}y^{4}}{2by^{3}}$ d) $\dfrac{8w^{3}a^{3}}{2w^{2}a}$ e) $\dfrac{6m^{6}x^{4}}{2m^{4}x}$ f) $\dfrac{9y^{5}m^{3}}{3ym}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $4p^{2}w^{2}$ b) $2w^{4}1$ c) $2b^{2}y$ d) $4wa^{2}$ e) $3m^{2}x^{3}$ f) $3y^{4}m^{2}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Multi variable expressions Coefficient division Subtraction of integers Grouping like terms
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-11} ### Power of a Power with Coefficient Expand and simplify. a) $(5b^{2})^{2}$ b) $(2w^{2})^{2}$ c) $(5c^{5})^{3}$ d) $(5m^{2})^{2}$ e) $(2y^{5})^{3}$ f) $(4k^{5})^{4}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $25b^{4}$ b) $4w^{4}$ c) $125c^{15}$ d) $25m^{4}$ e) $8y^{15}$ f) $256k^{20}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Power of power law Coefficient exponentiation Multiplication of integers
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-12} ### Power of a Power — Multi-variable Expand and simplify. a) $(2p^{2}k^{3})^{2}$ b) $(4aw^{3})^{2}$ c) $(3a^{2}w)^{2}$ d) $(4cb^{2})^{3}$ e) $(2bw)^{3}$ f) $(4w^{3}t^{3})^{2}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $4p^{4}k^{6}$ b) $16a^{2}w^{6}$ c) $9a^{4}w^{2}$ d) $64c^{3}b^{6}$ e) $8b^{3}w^{3}$ f) $16w^{6}t^{6}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Power of power law Coefficient exponentiation Multi variable expressions
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-14} ### Evaluate Negative Indices Evaluate. Write as a fraction. a) $4^{-1}$ b) $3^{-3}$ c) $4^{-2}$ d) $5^{-3}$ e) $7^{-1}$ f) $10^{-3}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $\dfrac{1}{4}$ b) $\dfrac{1}{27}$ c) $\dfrac{1}{16}$ d) $\dfrac{1}{125}$ e) $\dfrac{1}{7}$ f) $\dfrac{1}{1000}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Negative index as reciprocal Integer exponentiation Fraction notation
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-15} ### Negative Index — Fraction Base Evaluate. Simplify your answer. a) $\left(\dfrac{2}{3}\right)^{-1}$ b) $\left(\dfrac{2}{5}\right)^{-1}$ c) $\left(\dfrac{2}{3}\right)^{-3}$ d) $\left(\dfrac{4}{5}\right)^{-1}$ e) $\left(\dfrac{2}{5}\right)^{-2}$ f) $\left(\dfrac{4}{5}\right)^{-1}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $\dfrac{3}{2}$ b) $\dfrac{5}{2}$ c) $\dfrac{27}{8}$ d) $\dfrac{5}{4}$ e) $\dfrac{25}{4}$ f) $\dfrac{5}{4}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Negative index as reciprocal Fraction reciprocal Integer exponentiation
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-16} ### Simplify with Negative Indices Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $x^{3} \times x^{-3}$ b) $p^{5} \times p^{-3}$ c) $a^{2} \times a^{-2}$ d) $n^{2} \times n^{-5}$ e) $x^{3} \times x^{-3}$ f) $c^{5} \times c^{-1}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $1$ b) $p^{2}$ c) $1$ d) $n^{-3}$ e) $1$ f) $c^{4}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Addition of integers Negative integer addition Index notation
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-17} ### Division Giving Negative Index Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $y^{2} \div y^{3}$ b) $b^{3} \div b^{4}$ c) $x^{3} \div x^{7}$ d) $c^{2} \div c^{5}$ e) $x^{2} \div x^{5}$ f) $m^{3} \div m^{4}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $y^{-1}$ b) $b^{-1}$ c) $x^{-4}$ d) $c^{-3}$ e) $x^{-3}$ f) $m^{-1}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Subtraction of integers Negative index as reciprocal Index notation
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-18} ### Fractional Index — Unit Fraction Evaluate. a) $49^{\frac{1}{2}}$ b) $16^{\frac{1}{4}}$ c) $8^{\frac{1}{3}}$ d) $32^{\frac{1}{5}}$ e) $81^{\frac{1}{4}}$ f) $64^{\frac{1}{2}}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $7$ b) $2$ c) $2$ d) $2$ e) $3$ f) $8$ ::: ::: exercise-meta

::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-22} ### Complex Fraction Simplify. Give your answer in index form. a) $\dfrac{n^{3} \times n^{4}}{n}$ b) $\dfrac{b^{6} \times b^{5}}{b^{6}}$ c) $\dfrac{x^{5} \times x^{2}}{x^{4}}$ d) $\dfrac{t^{3} \times t^{4}}{t}$ e) $\dfrac{y^{2} \times y^{3}}{y^{3}}$ f) $\dfrac{y^{2} \times y^{4}}{y^{5}}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $n^{6}$ b) $b^{5}$ c) $x^{3}$ d) $t^{6}$ e) $y^{2}$ f) $y$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Combining multiplication and division laws Multi step simplification Index notation
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-26} ### Numeric Index Laws — Multiply Simplify. Give your answer as a single power. a) $3^{5} \times 3^{2}$ b) $3^{4} \times 3^{5}$ c) $5^{3} \times 5^{5}$ d) $2^{6} \times 2^{6}$ e) $2^{5} \times 2^{6}$ f) $5^{2} \times 5^{2}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $3^{7}$ b) $3^{9}$ c) $5^{8}$ d) $2^{12}$ e) $2^{11}$ f) $5^{4}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta

::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-27} ### Numeric Index Laws — Divide Simplify. Give your answer as a single power. a) $5^{7} \div 5^{5}$ b) $3^{4} \div 3^{3}$ c) $3^{8} \div 3^{5}$ d) $2^{8} \div 2^{5}$ e) $3^{8} \div 3^{5}$ f) $3^{5} \div 3^{4}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $5^{2}$ b) $3^{1}$ c) $3^{3}$ d) $2^{3}$ e) $3^{3}$ f) $3^{1}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Numeric base recognition Subtraction of integers Expressing as single power
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-29} ### Write as Power of n Write as a single power of the given base. a) $\text{Write } 1 \text{ as a power of } 5$ b) $\text{Write } \frac{1}{2} \text{ as a power of } 2$ c) $\text{Write } 16 \text{ as a power of } 2$ d) $\text{Write } \frac{1}{2} \text{ as a power of } 2$ e) $\text{Write } 125 \text{ as a power of } 5$ f) $\text{Write } 16 \text{ as a power of } 2$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $5^{0}$ b) $2^{-1}$ c) $2^{4}$ d) $2^{-1}$ e) $5^{3}$ f) $2^{4}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Prime factorisation Index notation Negative index as reciprocal Zero exponent rule
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-30} ### Find Missing Index Find the missing index. a) $t^{4} \times t^{\square} = t^{6}$ b) $t^{5} \times t^{\square} = t^{12}$ c) $y^{3} \times y^{\square} = y^{10}$ d) $b^{7} \times b^{\square} = b^{13}$ e) $w^{2} \times w^{\square} = w^{5}$ f) $n^{6} \times n^{\square} = n^{13}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $2$ b) $7$ c) $7$ d) $6$ e) $3$ f) $7$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Reverse index law Subtraction of integers Algebraic reasoning
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-31} ### Find Missing Index — Division Find the missing index. a) $\dfrac{b^{11}}{b^{\square}} = b^{6}$ b) $\dfrac{x^{14}}{x^{\square}} = x^{9}$ c) $\dfrac{w^{7}}{w^{\square}} = w^{4}$ d) $\dfrac{p^{7}}{p^{\square}} = p^{4}$ e) $\dfrac{y^{9}}{y^{\square}} = y^{5}$ f) $\dfrac{y^{12}}{y^{\square}} = y^{9}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $5$ b) $5$ c) $3$ d) $3$ e) $4$ f) $3$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Reverse index law Subtraction of integers Algebraic reasoning
::: ::::: ::: math-sep ∑ × π ÷ √ ◆ ∞ ± ∫ ≠ Δ ::: ### 🔴 Challenging ::::: {#exr-u2-4} ### Fractional Index — Non-unit Evaluate. a) $25^{\frac{3}{2}}$ b) $27^{\frac{2}{3}}$ c) $25^{\frac{3}{2}}$ d) $25^{\frac{3}{2}}$ e) $16^{\frac{3}{2}}$ f) $125^{\frac{2}{3}}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $125$ b) $9$ c) $125$ d) $125$ e) $64$ f) $25$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Fractional index as root and power Nth root evaluation Integer exponentiation Order of operations
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-13} ### Negative Outer Power Simplify. Give your answer with positive exponents. a) $(4w^{3})^{-1}$ b) $(3n^{2})^{-3}$ c) $(3w^{3})^{-2}$ d) $(2b^{3})^{-3}$ e) $(2t^{2})^{-3}$ f) $(4m^{3})^{-1}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $\dfrac{1}{4w^{3}}$ b) $\dfrac{1}{27n^{6}}$ c) $\dfrac{1}{9w^{6}}$ d) $\dfrac{1}{8b^{9}}$ e) $\dfrac{1}{8t^{6}}$ f) $\dfrac{1}{4m^{3}}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Power of power law Negative index as reciprocal Coefficient exponentiation
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-19} ### Negative Fractional Index Evaluate. a) $32^{\frac{-4}{5}}$ b) $25^{\frac{-1}{2}}$ c) $81^{\frac{-3}{4}}$ d) $81^{\frac{-3}{4}}$ e) $64^{\frac{-1}{3}}$ f) $81^{\frac{-3}{4}}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $1/16$ b) $1/5$ c) $1/27$ d) $1/27$ e) $1/4$ f) $1/27$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Fractional index as root and power Negative index as reciprocal Nth root evaluation Combining index laws
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-20} ### Fractional Index of a Fraction Evaluate. Simplify your answer. a) $\left(\dfrac{9}{25}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}$ b) $\left(\dfrac{4}{9}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}$ c) $\left(\dfrac{16}{25}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}$ d) $\left(\dfrac{9}{25}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}$ e) $\left(\dfrac{27}{1000}\right)^{\frac{2}{3}}$ f) $\left(\dfrac{4}{9}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $27/125$ b) $2/3$ c) $4/5$ d) $27/125$ e) $9/100$ f) $2/3$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Fractional index as root and power Fraction exponentiation Nth root of fraction
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-23} ### Complex Fraction with Coefficients Simplify fully. a) $\dfrac{3x^{2} \times 3x^{4}}{9x^{3}}$ b) $\dfrac{2p^{3} \times 4p^{2}}{8p^{4}}$ c) $\dfrac{3n^{5} \times 4n^{4}}{12n}$ d) $\dfrac{4w^{4} \times 3w^{3}}{3w^{4}}$ e) $\dfrac{3w^{2} \times 2w^{3}}{2w^{3}}$ f) $\dfrac{3c^{2} \times 3c^{2}}{3c}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $x^{3}$ b) $p$ c) $n^{8}$ d) $4w^{3}$ e) $3w^{2}$ f) $3c^{3}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Coefficient division Combining multiplication and division laws Multi step simplification Separating coefficients from indices
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-24} ### Complex Fraction — Multi-variable Simplify fully. a) $\dfrac{8y^{6}p^{4}}{4y^{4}p}$ b) $\dfrac{8b^{6}k^{2}}{4b^{2}k}$ c) $\dfrac{16b^{5}p^{4}}{4b^{2}p^{2}}$ d) $\dfrac{6p^{8}y^{3}}{3p^{4}y}$ e) $\dfrac{6w^{6}n^{5}}{3w^{4}n^{3}}$ f) $\dfrac{4n^{6}m^{5}}{2n^{3}m^{2}}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $2y^{2}p^{3}$ b) $2b^{4}k$ c) $4b^{3}p^{2}$ d) $2p^{4}y^{2}$ e) $2w^{2}n^{2}$ f) $2n^{3}m^{3}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Multi variable expressions Coefficient division Multi step simplification Grouping like terms
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-25} ### Nested Power in Fraction Simplify fully. a) $\dfrac{c^{3} \times (c^{3})^{2}}{c^{4}}$ b) $\dfrac{b^{5} \times (b^{3})^{3}}{b^{9}}$ c) $\dfrac{k^{5} \times (k^{2})^{2}}{k^{7}}$ d) $\dfrac{y^{2} \times (y^{4})^{3}}{y^{8}}$ e) $\dfrac{a^{4} \times (a^{3})^{3}}{a^{7}}$ f) $\dfrac{p^{3} \times (p^{3})^{2}}{p^{5}}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $c^{5}$ b) $b^{5}$ c) $k^{2}$ d) $y^{6}$ e) $a^{6}$ f) $p^{4}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Power of power law Combining multiplication and division laws Multi step simplification Order of operations
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-28} ### Numeric Index Laws — Mixed Simplify. Give your answer as a single power. a) $\dfrac{3^{4} \times 3^{-1}}{3^{1}}$ b) $\dfrac{3^{5} \times 3^{-1}}{3^{1}}$ c) $\dfrac{5^{4} \times 5^{-3}}{5^{2}}$ d) $\dfrac{2^{4} \times 2^{-1}}{2^{2}}$ e) $\dfrac{2^{5} \times 2^{-2}}{2^{1}}$ f) $\dfrac{5^{5} \times 5^{-2}}{5^{3}}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $3^{2}$ b) $3^{3}$ c) $5^{-1}$ d) $2^{1}$ e) $2^{2}$ f) $5^{0}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Numeric base recognition Combining multiplication and division laws Negative integer addition Multi step simplification
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-32} ### Find Missing Power Find the missing power. a) $(k^{3})^{\square} = k^{9}$ b) $(c^{4})^{\square} = c^{16}$ c) $(p^{5})^{\square} = p^{15}$ d) $(y^{4})^{\square} = y^{12}$ e) $(m^{2})^{\square} = m^{6}$ f) $(k^{3})^{\square} = k^{9}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $3$ b) $4$ c) $3$ d) $3$ e) $3$ f) $3$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
Reverse power of power law Division of integers Algebraic reasoning
::: ::::: ::::: {#exr-u2-33} ### Identify and Correct Error Identify the mistake and write the correct answer. a) $27^{\frac{1}{3}} = 9\text{ (claim: } \frac{1}{3} \text{ of } 27 = 9\text{). Correct?}$ b) $27^{\frac{1}{3}} = 9\text{ (claim: } \frac{1}{3} \text{ of } 27 = 9\text{). Correct?}$ c) $27^{\frac{1}{3}} = 9\text{ (claim: } \frac{1}{3} \text{ of } 27 = 9\text{). Correct?}$ d) $9^{-2} = -81\text{. Is this correct? Explain.}$ e) $9^{-2} = -81\text{. Is this correct? Explain.}$ f) $y^8 \times y^3 = y^{24}\text{. Explain the mistake.}$ ::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"} ## 🔍 View Solution a) $\text{No. } 27^{\frac{1}{3}} = \sqrt[3]{27} = 3$ b) $\text{No. } 27^{\frac{1}{3}} = \sqrt[3]{27} = 3$ c) $\text{No. } 27^{\frac{1}{3}} = \sqrt[3]{27} = 3$ d) $\text{No. } 9^{-2} = \dfrac{1}{9^2} = \dfrac{1}{81}$ e) $\text{No. } 9^{-2} = \dfrac{1}{9^2} = \dfrac{1}{81}$ f) $\text{Powers should be added, not multiplied. Correct: } y^{8+3} = y^{11}$ ::: ::: exercise-meta
